title: Issue Card v1
description: One JSON per logged issue — a bug, defect, incident, or open question. This is the place to track issues when there is no external tracker (Jira/GitHub), or to keep a personal record of issues that matter to you. The defining link is action_cards[]: an issue is observed and triaged here, and the WORK it requires lives as action-cards that follow from it. Severity (technical impact) and priority (business urgency) are tracked separately, per standard issue-tracking practice. Lives in <project>/issues/.

id.description: Format: ISS-{customer_code}-{project_code}-{NNNN}. Four digits — issues accumulate. Example: ISS-ACME-CLOUD-0001.

type.description: Kind of issue. bug/defect=something broken; incident=live production disruption; question=needs an answer/clarification; improvement=enhancement request. Note: a plain unit of work is NOT an issue — that is an action-card. An issue is something observed (broken, unclear, asked) from which work may follow via action_cards[].

severity.description: TECHNICAL impact — how badly it breaks things, independent of business urgency.

priority.description: BUSINESS urgency — how soon it needs handling. Deliberately separate from severity: a high-severity crash on an unused screen can be low priority; a cosmetic typo on the homepage can be high priority.

status.description: Workflow state. resolved=a resolution has been applied and is awaiting verification; closed=verified/accepted and done.

environment.description: Where it occurs, e.g. 'Northwind test env', 'prod, OrderHub 3.2'. Most useful for bugs/incidents.

steps_to_reproduce.description: For bugs/defects: the steps that trigger it.

resolution.description: How it was closed out. Set when status reaches resolved/closed.

action_cards.description: Action-cards that follow from this issue — the work to investigate, fix, or answer it. This is the loop: log the issue here, drive the work as action-cards.

sources.description: Where the issue came from (a log file, an email, an external tracker id, a meeting).

external_ref.description: Id in an external tracker if this is also logged there (e.g. 'PROJ-1234'). Keeps the personal card and the official ticket linked.

updated.description: Date this card was last touched.
